favorable_conditions: Warm temperatures, high humidity, rainfall splash, and overhead irrigation.
diagnosis_notes: Small dark lesions with yellow halos on leaves, with raised scabby fruit spots in advanced stages.
disease_description: Bacterial Spot is a contagious bacterial disease that weakens foliage and reduces fruit quality.
symptoms: Dark angular to irregular leaf spots, yellowing around lesions, shot-hole appearance, and fruit blemishes.
causes: Often introduced through contaminated seed/transplants and spread by splashing water and field handling.
prevention_methods: Use certified clean seed, avoid overhead irrigation, disinfect tools, and rotate away from host crops.
treatment_methods: Remove heavily infected tissue and apply crop-approved bactericide programs guided by local recommendations.
treatment_organic: Sanitation pruning, drip irrigation, mulch splash barriers, and copper-based organic options.
treatment_chemical: Copper + partner bactericide rotations according to crop label and resistance guidance.
recovery_time: Leaf recovery is limited; focus is on containing spread and protecting new growth.
monitoring_tips: Track new leaf infections after rainfall and inspect fruit lesions twice weekly.
